projectDescription: A pedagogical IDE that uses Claude Opus as a Socratic interlocutor to guide students through specification-first programming, helping instructors observe how students reason — not just whether their code runs.
keyFeatures: Spec-first workflow: students write a specification before any code, with Opus blocking progress until the spec is complete
Socratic coding assistant: Opus answers syntax questions directly but returns counter-questions for reasoning gaps
Intent-diff review: Opus compares the student's spec against their final code and surfaces neutral divergence questions
Live instructor dashboard: real-time per-student summaries generated by Opus, streamed via Server-Sent Events
Per-session reasoning view: two-column view showing student work alongside Opus's private predictions and alignment signals
Cohort analytics: post-exercise narrative summarising common drifts, strengths, and curriculum suggestions
Fast exercise authoring: instructor writes a plain-text prompt, Opus returns reviewable scaffolding in seconds
Bilingual support (English/Spanish) with on-demand translation caching
Monaco editor with autocomplete explicitly disabled to encourage deliberate coding
publicApiSurface: POST /api/author — generate exercise scaffolding from an instructor prompt
POST /api/session/[sid]/spec — submit a spec draft for Opus review (Phase 1)
POST /api/session/[sid]/chat — send a Phase 2 chat message to Opus
POST /api/session/[sid]/submit — finalise Phase 2 code and trigger intent-diff
POST /api/session/[sid]/divergence-response — submit student answer to a divergence question
POST /api/session/[sid]/revise — record optional post-review code revision
GET  /api/live — Server-Sent Events stream for the instructor dashboard
GET  /api/cohort/[id] — cohort session data and narrative
